title: aspidium dubrueillianum
intro: Aspidium dubrueillianum, also known as the Leptopteris dubrueillianum, is a species of fern native to New Caledonia, a region in the Pacific Ocean. It is a type of vascular plant, specifically a member of the family Dryopteridaceae. Aspidium dubrueillianum has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which have been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health.
